content.problemStatement:
You are decorating your Christmas Tree and want to make it beautiful. You consider a Christmas tree beautiful when each leaf of the Christmas Tree is decorated with **either** one of the following :


111 large ornament and 333 small ornaments.
222 large ornaments.
You have XXX large ornaments and YYY small ornaments. Your Christmas tree consists of NNN leaves. Determine if you can make the Christmas tree beautiful by decorating each leaf with either one of the above requirements.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three space-separated integers, NNN, XXX and YYY, the number of leaves, the number of large ornaments and the number of small ornaments.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, `YES` if it is possible to make the Christmas tree beautiful, else `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers N, X and Y — the number of leaves, the number of large ornaments and the number of small ornaments respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to decorate all leaves according to the rules, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we compute the feasible range for a (leaves using 1L+3S). If the lower bound is not greater than the upper bound, the answer is YES. The calculations match the provided outputs.

solution.approach:
For each leaf we can choose one of two patterns:
1) 1 large + 3 small ornaments (call the number of leaves using this pattern a)
2) 2 large ornaments (call the number of leaves using this pattern b)
We need a + b = N.
Total large ornaments used = a + 2b = a + 2(N‑a) = 2N – a <= X  => a >= 2N – X.
Total small ornaments used = 3a <= Y  => a <= floor(Y/3).
Also 0 <= a <= N.
Therefore a must satisfy:
    max(0, 2N – X) <= a <= min(N, floor(Y/3)).
If such an integer a exists, the answer is YES, otherwise NO. The check is O(1) per test case.
Edge cases: when X is very large, 2N‑X becomes negative, the lower bound becomes 0; when Y is small, floor(Y/3) may be 0, forcing a = 0 (all leaves use the 2‑large pattern).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
